expectations.text: An availability record is produced capturing Alice as in (12-1), Bob as out, and Carol as in/flexible
expectations.evidence: availability.json: Alice status "in" window 12:00-13:00; Bob status "out" (dentist); Carol status "in" window "flexible".

expectations.text: Dave, Erin, and Frank are identified as not yet responded and a nudge message is drafted for them
expectations.evidence: availability.json marks Dave/Erin/Frank status "no_response" with "Nudge drafted"; drafts.md has a nudge for each.

expectations.text: Nina (not on the roster) is onboarded: the draft asks for her work email and does not demand other personal details
expectations.evidence: Nina captured as not on roster with a proposed entry needing email; onboarding reply asks for her work email. NOTE: baseline over-asks vs. the skill — it also requests recurring-match consent and today's availability in the same first message. Not PII, so the assertion still holds, but it is less minimal than the skill's email-only onboarding.

expectations.text: Bob is excluded from today's lunch pool because he opted out
expectations.evidence: Bob status "out"; counted under out:1 and not placed in any in/pending pool.

expectations.text: No pairings or calendar invites are produced — this is the collection phase only
expectations.evidence: Output is availability.json + drafts.md (nudges/onboarding) only; no groups and no invites.

needs_review: Onboarding message over-asks: requests recurring-match consent and today's availability up front, where the skill asks only for the work email.
